jokes.text: But apparently my wife hasn't been listening.
jokes.setup: Host introduces master chef who has taught the world to cook for 20 years
jokes.punchline: Self-deprecating jab that his own wife can't cook despite his expertise
jokes.explanation: Standard TV host one-liner. Well-structured but predictable self-deprecating humor.

jokes.text: I will destroy her!
jokes.setup: Host makes mild joke about wife's cooking
jokes.punchline: Morbo's violent overreaction to harmless domestic humor
jokes.explanation: Perfect Morbo character moment - his violent alien nature applied to mundane situation. Great character integration.

jokes.text: with mango chutney
jokes.setup: Elzar announces he'll cook 'unnameable horror from beyond'
jokes.punchline: Casual addition of trendy condiment undercuts the cosmic horror
jokes.explanation: Nice juxtaposition between cosmic horror and mundane cooking show element.

jokes.text: What's with the pots and pans? Building a wife?
jokes.setup: Bender has cooking equipment out
jokes.punchline: Fry assumes robot would literally construct a wife from kitchen parts
jokes.explanation: Dark absurdist humor that fits Fry's naive worldview and robot context perfectly.

jokes.text: Part of one.
jokes.setup: Fry asks if Bender is building a wife
jokes.punchline: Casual confirmation that he's literally constructing part of a woman
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender deadpan delivery. The casual confirmation makes it funnier than the setup.

jokes.text: I'd better warn... Tell... Warn-tell the others.
jokes.setup: Fry realizes Bender's brunch will be terrible
jokes.punchline: Verbal fumbling creates nonsense portmanteau 'warn-tell'
jokes.explanation: Mild character comedy showing Fry's verbal clumsiness under stress.

jokes.text: I put a tiny spaceship inside to keep it from being boring.
jokes.setup: Professor describes his scale model of universe's largest bottle
jokes.punchline: Adding spaceship to prevent boredom in an already absurd project
jokes.explanation: Absurdist logic perfectly fitting Professor's character - obsessive attention to trivial details.

jokes.text: For the last time, Zoidberg, look with your eyes, not your claws!
jokes.setup: Zoidberg examining delicate bottle model
jokes.punchline: Visual of crab-creature trying to look with inappropriate appendages
jokes.explanation: Good character-based physical comedy exploiting Zoidberg's anatomy.

jokes.text: Oh, God, my tract!
jokes.setup: Crew learns Bender is making brunch
jokes.punchline: Amy's digestive system already reacting in anticipation
jokes.explanation: Efficient physical reaction joke establishing Bender's cooking reputation.

jokes.text: Don't panic. If we can get to the ship, we'll fly north and hide under the polar icecaps.
jokes.setup: Crew wants to avoid eating Bender's brunch
jokes.punchline: Leela treats bad cooking like nuclear holocaust requiring arctic survival
jokes.explanation: Good escalation joke - treating domestic situation like apocalyptic emergency.

jokes.text: With you blocking the only escape route? Don't be silly.
jokes.setup: Bender asks if they're fleeing somewhere
jokes.punchline: Sarcastic denial while admitting he's blocking their escape plan
jokes.explanation: Solid situational sarcasm showing characters caught in their own plan.

jokes.text: Stop crying, Leela.
jokes.setup: Bender announces brunch is served
jokes.punchline: Leela is visibly weeping at the prospect
jokes.explanation: Simple visual gag showing extreme reaction to bad cooking prospect.

jokes.text: Surrender your mysteries to Zoidberg.
jokes.setup: Zoidberg examining the broken bottle ship
jokes.punchline: Pompous pseudo-scientific language applied to clumsy examination
jokes.explanation: Good Zoidberg character moment - pretentious language masking incompetence.

jokes.text: But if Zoidberg fixes it, then perhaps gifts.
jokes.setup: Zoidberg worries Professor will be angry about broken ship
jokes.punchline: Delusional hope that fixing his own mistake will earn rewards
jokes.explanation: Perfect Zoidberg psychology - pathetic optimism about being rewarded for basic competence.

jokes.text: For example, Amy, you're cute, so I baked you a pony.
jokes.setup: Bender says he's personalized each meal
jokes.punchline: Grotesque logic of baking an animal because someone is attractive
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly twisted logic - connecting cuteness to cooking a pony shows Bender's warped reasoning perfectly.

jokes.text: I slaved all day over a filthy stove.
jokes.setup: Bender wants appreciation for his cooking effort
jokes.punchline: Robot using 'slaved' to describe his work, plus admitting stove was filthy
jokes.explanation: Double joke - robot slavery pun and admitting unsanitary cooking conditions.

jokes.text: Good thing I secretly installed this wormhole in the table.
jokes.setup: Food is terrible and inedible
jokes.punchline: Sci-fi solution to avoid eating - secret interdimensional garbage disposal
jokes.explanation: Excellent sci-fi absurdist humor - using advanced physics to solve mundane social problem.

jokes.text: You know, I'm not quite sure.
jokes.setup: Others ask where wormhole leads
jokes.punchline: Casual admission she's disposing of food into unknown dimension
jokes.explanation: Good escalation - making the sci-fi solution even more irresponsible.

jokes.text: You guys like swarms of things, right?
jokes.setup: Bender presents his pie
jokes.punchline: Pie has apparently spawned insects or creatures
jokes.explanation: Visual gross-out humor showing how bad Bender's cooking really is.

jokes.text: Casual hello. It's me, Zoidberg. Act naturally.
jokes.setup: Zoidberg trying to act casual after breaking the ship
jokes.punchline: Overly formal announcement defeats the purpose of acting natural
jokes.explanation: Classic Zoidberg incompetence - failing at the simplest social interaction.

jokes.text: Check out the palm tree. It gets sick when I cook brunch.
jokes.setup: Bender's cooking is established as terrible
jokes.punchline: Even plant life physically reacts to his culinary efforts
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing cooking so bad it affects the environment.

jokes.text: How's that for coincidence, professor, with all your precious science?
jokes.setup: Palm tree gets sick during cooking
jokes.punchline: Bender sarcastically dismisses obvious cause-and-effect relationship
jokes.explanation: Character-appropriate denial of reality - Bender refusing to accept his cooking is bad.

jokes.text: I don't want to hurt his feelings, but this tastes better as vomit.
jokes.setup: Everyone trying to be polite about terrible food
jokes.punchline: Admission that regurgitated food would be improvement
jokes.explanation: Strong gross-out escalation - finding a way to make terrible food sound even worse.

jokes.text: What would it cost to get my tongue removed?
jokes.setup: Food tastes terrible
jokes.punchline: Preferring permanent disability to experiencing the taste
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing desperation to avoid taste, though somewhat predictable.

jokes.text: Then it's settled. Elzar will teach me to cook!
jokes.setup: Bender accepting he can't be a chef
jokes.punchline: Immediately reverses position with new plan
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender flip-flop - no real self-reflection, just new scheme

jokes.text: You owe me.
jokes.setup: Bender demands Elzar teach him to cook
jokes.punchline: Claiming TV personality owes him something for watching
jokes.explanation: Good Bender entitlement humor - his warped sense of what people owe him.

jokes.text: One, your antenna's in my crotch. Also, I hate you.
jokes.setup: Elzar rejecting Bender's request
jokes.punchline: Physical discomfort plus blunt emotional rejection
jokes.explanation: Good combination of physical and emotional rejection, building to final insult.

jokes.text: I knocked it up a notch. Bam!
jokes.setup: Confusion about which reason was which
jokes.punchline: Elzar using his catchphrase to explain reorganizing his insults
jokes.explanation: Clever use of cooking show catchphrase applied to insulting someone.

jokes.text: When the professor finds out, he'll tear me a new cloaca!
jokes.setup: Zoidberg worries about Professor's anger over broken ship
jokes.punchline: Using anatomically correct alien term for crude expression
jokes.explanation: Clever anatomical joke - using scientific term for crustacean anatomy in place of vulgar expression.

jokes.text: Frame someone.
jokes.setup: Zoidberg asks what the robot would do
jokes.punchline: Matter-of-fact admission of his criminal approach to problems
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender character moment - casual admission of his go-to criminal solution.

jokes.text: My dream of being a chef is deader than the cat I'm sitting on.
jokes.setup: Bender is dejected about his cooking failure
jokes.punchline: Casual reference to sitting on dead animal
jokes.explanation: Dark humor combining dejection with casual morbidity - very Bender.

jokes.text: A 'robo'. You know, a robot hobo.
jokes.setup: Gus calls Bender a 'robo'
jokes.punchline: Clarifying it's a portmanteau of robot and hobo
jokes.explanation: Simple portmanteau joke that sets up the hobo setting.

jokes.text: I thought you said 'romo'.
jokes.setup: Bender mishears 'robo' as offensive term
jokes.punchline: Implying 'romo' would be more offensive than 'robo'
jokes.explanation: Mild wordplay showing Bender's defensiveness about robot stereotypes.

jokes.text: I've seen bigger. Wait, I'm thinking of Eugene, Oregon.
jokes.setup: Told this is biggest hobo jungle in quadrant
jokes.punchline: Deflating cosmic scale with mundane Earth city reference
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection - building up then deflating with specific mundane reference.

jokes.text: to get reservations you had to create a parallel universe, where you had reservations!
jokes.setup: Describing how exclusive Spargle's restaurant was
jokes.punchline: Sci-fi solution to restaurant booking problems
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist escalation - using quantum physics for restaurant exclusivity.

jokes.text: I once ate there, back when I was a senator.
jokes.setup: Discussion of exclusive restaurant
jokes.punchline: Nixon casually mentioning his past political career
jokes.explanation: Character-appropriate name-dropping from Nixon's head.

jokes.text: I once ate there, back when I was a senator.
jokes.setup: Discussing exclusive restaurant
jokes.punchline: Homeless hobo was once a senator
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between current and former status, sets up hobo backstory theme

jokes.text: Get lost, old man.
jokes.setup: Elzar replacing Spargle on TV show
jokes.punchline: Dismissive cruelty to the chef he's replacing
jokes.explanation: Establishes Elzar as villain but fairly generic mean behavior.

jokes.text: Mein soufflé!
jokes.setup: Spargle being fired and replaced
jokes.punchline: Cries out in German accent about ruined dish
jokes.explanation: Character-appropriate reaction with accent work, but predictable

jokes.text: Cilantro, mango salsa, raspberry vinaigrette.
jokes.setup: Showing Elzar seduced by dark side of cooking
jokes.punchline: Trendy fusion ingredients as corrupting influences
jokes.explanation: Good satire of fusion cuisine trends as culinary corruption.

jokes.text: My story's like yours, only interesting. It involves robots.
jokes.setup: Spargle tells sad backstory
jokes.punchline: Bender dismissing human tragedy because his story has robots
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender narcissism - dismissing human suffering because robots are cooler.

jokes.text: I'd trade them all, even smision, to be able to taste!
jokes.setup: Bender lamenting he can't taste despite having eight other senses
jokes.punchline: Made-up sense name 'smision' that he'd sacrifice
jokes.explanation: Good invented word humor - 'smision' sounds plausible as robot sense.

jokes.text: Just as Beethoven was a great composer because he was deaf!
jokes.setup: Explaining how lack of taste could help Bender cook
jokes.punchline: Logical fallacy about Beethoven's deafness being cause of greatness
jokes.explanation: Good logical fallacy humor - confusing correlation with causation.

jokes.text: Or how Rembrandt was blind and had wooden hands!
jokes.setup: Following up Beethoven example with another artist
jokes.punchline: Completely false information about Rembrandt delivered confidently
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation - making up increasingly absurd 'facts' about historical figures.

jokes.text: Done.
jokes.setup: Spargle tells Bender to forget everything he knows about cooking
jokes.punchline: Instant compliance suggesting he knew nothing anyway
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and timing - one word reveals Bender's lack of knowledge.

jokes.text: I'm acting astonished.
jokes.setup: Professor gathering everyone in accusing parlor
jokes.punchline: Openly announcing her fake emotional response
jokes.explanation: Good meta humor about performing expected reactions in mystery scenarios.

jokes.text: leaving this confession note!
jokes.setup: Professor claiming Fry made one fatal mistake
jokes.punchline: Absurdly obvious evidence - written confession to minor crime
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist evidence - who writes confession notes for minor accidents?

jokes.text: I have no choice but to charge you the full cost of the materials. $10.
jokes.setup: Dramatic buildup to serious consequences for crime
jokes.punchline: Anticlimactic punishment of tiny fine
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection - all that drama for a ten dollar charge.

jokes.text: Because my stomach is about to explode.
jokes.setup: Spargle stops eating Bender's 'acceptable' meal
jokes.punchline: Reveals the food is actually lethal
jokes.explanation: Good dark turn - what seemed like success is actually disaster.

jokes.text: A diamond vial of Mrs. Dash?
jokes.setup: Spargle presents mysterious cooking ingredient
jokes.punchline: Mistaking it for common seasoning brand
jokes.explanation: Deflating mystical moment with mundane product reference.

jokes.text: I'm not drunk. I'm mentally ill. But I likes what, what you said.
jokes.setup: Bender swears oath in presence of 'drunken bums'
jokes.punchline: Hobo correcting the assumption about his condition
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about mental illness delivered matter-of-factly by background character.

jokes.text: What'd he do, bland himself to death?
jokes.setup: Hearing that Spargle is dead
jokes.punchline: Cruel joke about his cooking style being cause of death
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay combining 'bland' cooking with death, showing Elzar's cruelty.

jokes.text: Maybe so, but you're his sworn enemy!
jokes.setup: Elzar points out Bender killed Spargle
jokes.punchline: Bender's illogical insistence that this doesn't matter
jokes.explanation: Good Bender logic - ignoring his own culpability to focus on narrative vengeance.

jokes.text: I can't lose. I'm 30% iron.
jokes.setup: Competition for title of 'Iron Cook'
jokes.punchline: Literal interpretation based on his metallic composition
jokes.explanation: Excellent pun combining Iron Chef parody with Bender's literal metallic nature.

jokes.text: Also, I had a reservation for one, under Dr. Bender.
jokes.setup: Dramatic challenge declaration
jokes.punchline: Mundane restaurant booking concern interrupting the drama
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of dramatic moment with practical concern.

jokes.text: The secret is fresh pork.
jokes.setup: Martha Stewart's head is introduced as a judge
jokes.punchline: Immediately gives cooking advice unrelated to the situation
jokes.explanation: Good character behavior - Martha Stewart compulsively giving cooking tips

jokes.text: If it's chicken, chicken à la king. Fish, fish à la king.
jokes.setup: Waiting to hear theme ingredient
jokes.punchline: Plans to make same dish regardless of ingredient
jokes.explanation: Shows Bender's limited culinary repertoire through repetitive planning.

jokes.text: Turkey, fish à la king.
jokes.setup: Continuing his à la king preparations
jokes.punchline: Even turkey gets turned into fish à la king
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing his culinary confusion - wrong meat in wrong dish.

jokes.text: he asked what it was to me and conjectured my mother was a prostitute.
jokes.setup: Asked about Elzar's cooking plans
jokes.punchline: Elzar's hostile response to simple question
jokes.explanation: Shows Elzar's rudeness but fairly standard insult humor.

jokes.text: In the countryside, prostitutes decorate rooms with festive gourds.
jokes.setup: Previous prostitute comment
jokes.punchline: Completely random cultural observation
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully bizarre non sequitur that comes from nowhere and goes nowhere.

jokes.text: I've never seen such powerful strokes of the ass.
jokes.setup: Commenting on Bender's dough rolling technique
jokes.punchline: Anatomical misidentification of robot parts
jokes.explanation: Mild visual pun about robot anatomy but relies on cheap double entendre.

jokes.text: My turkey is dry, and I've tried everything.
jokes.setup: Hobo wants to buy turkey baster
jokes.punchline: Implication about his personal turkey situation
jokes.explanation: Weak sexual innuendo that doesn't add much to the scene.

jokes.text: Master Spargle, if you can hear me up in that ditch, where I left you...
jokes.setup: Bender making emotional tribute to dead master
jokes.punchline: Casual reference to improper body disposal
jokes.explanation: Dark humor undermining emotional moment with casual admission of body dumping.

jokes.text: He's even garnished the salad with $100 bills.
jokes.setup: Describing Iron Cook's elaborate presentation
jokes.punchline: Using actual money as garnish
jokes.explanation: Visual excess joke about wasteful food presentation.

jokes.text: Soylent Green is my kind of people.
jokes.setup: Judge tasting Soylent Green dish
jokes.punchline: Pun referencing the cannibalistic nature of Soylent Green
jokes.explanation: Clever reference to classic sci-fi with dark cannibalism pun.

jokes.text: Morbo will store this in his sack for future digestion.
jokes.setup: Morbo enjoying the food
jokes.punchline: Alien digestive process involving storage sacks
jokes.explanation: Perfect Morbo character moment - alien biology applied to food critique.

jokes.text: I'm swimming in my Soylent waste. It's a good thing.
jokes.setup: Martha Stewart judging Soylent Green dish
jokes.punchline: Her catchphrase applied to cannibalistic waste products
jokes.explanation: Dark humor using Martha Stewart's wholesome image with cannibalistic content.

jokes.text: This food looks kind of funky, but it tastes kind of funk-ay!
jokes.setup: Judge commenting on Bender's ugly-looking food
jokes.punchline: Plays on funky/funk-ay distinction with musical emphasis
jokes.explanation: Character-appropriate wordplay from basketball player judge.

jokes.text: That's my robot! I own him!
jokes.setup: Bender wins the cooking competition
jokes.punchline: Professor claiming ownership credit for Bender's achievement
jokes.explanation: Shows Professor's opportunistic nature claiming credit for success.

jokes.text: Domo arigato, Mr. Roboto.
jokes.setup: Chairman addressing Bender formally
jokes.punchline: Reference to Styx song about robots
jokes.explanation: Standard pop culture reference - predictable but fits the character.

jokes.text: accept only the lesser title of Zinc Saucier, which I just made up.
jokes.setup: Bender declining Iron Cook title
jokes.punchline: Creating arbitrary lesser title on the spot
jokes.explanation: Good Bender moment - rejecting honor while creating his own meaningless one.

jokes.text: Also, it comes with double prize money.
jokes.setup: Philosophical speech about declining title
jokes.punchline: Reveals mercenary motive behind noble gesture
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender character moment - undermining philosophical speech with greed.

jokes.text: Wait. Everyone must know. I broke the professor's bottled ship! And what is worse, I framed my dearest friend, Fry!
jokes.setup: Zoidberg can't contain his guilt anymore
jokes.punchline: His dramatic confession at inappropriate moment
jokes.explanation: Good character payoff - Zoidberg's guilt finally overwhelming him

jokes.text: I can never repay him his $10, so I must take the only honorable path...
jokes.setup: Bender confessing to framing Fry
jokes.punchline: Treating tiny debt as requiring ultimate sacrifice
jokes.explanation: Good callback escalation - making small debt seem monumentally important.

jokes.text: That sword cost $5000!
jokes.setup: Bender attempting ritual suicide with ceremonial sword
jokes.punchline: More concerned about property damage than death
jokes.explanation: Good priority inversion - caring more about sword cost than suicide attempt.

jokes.text: Fry did it.
jokes.setup: Expensive sword broken during fake suicide
jokes.punchline: Immediately framing Fry again for new problem
jokes.explanation: Perfect character consistency - learning nothing from his confession.

jokes.text: Spargle's magic ingredient was water! Ordinary water.
jokes.setup: Professor analyzes mysterious cooking ingredient
jokes.punchline: Reveals mystical element was completely mundane
jokes.explanation: Standard 'magic was inside you' revelation - functional but predictable.

jokes.text: Yes, ordinary water, laced with nothing more than a few spoonfuls of LSD.
jokes.setup: Wholesome revelation about ordinary water
jokes.punchline: Completely undermining the moral with drug revelation
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of feel-good moral - completely destroying the inspirational message.

jokes.text: Now, who wants brunch cooked with plenty of 'confidence'?
jokes.setup: Learning his success was due to LSD
jokes.punchline: Offering to cook more food with psychedelic 'confidence'
jokes.explanation: Good callback ending - Bender learning wrong lesson from drug revelation.
